mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth (e.g., >100k jobs/month for 3 months) but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates significantly on a year-over-year basis, it signals potential central bank tightening or erosion of purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth accelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising inflation can lead to higher interest rates and lower equity valuations.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers occur, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) show strong growth while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and strong sales typically support equity markets.
failure_condition: If the combined trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon, particularly during recessionary periods.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment is a classic recessionary indicator.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises as specified during a declared recession, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short, one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a clear upward trend (e.g., 3 consecutive months of >5% MoM growth) but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production (INDPRO) growth indicates strong manufacturing and mining activity, signaling healthy corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production reflects the health of the manufacturing sector.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth meets the trigger condition, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy, which typically increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates generally negatively impact equity valuations.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases as specified, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth is slowing while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is simultaneously rising, it indicates a significant economic deceleration or recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.0% AND UNRATE 3-month average change > 0.2%.
evidence_refs: fact:Combined slowing growth and rising unemployment are strong recession signals.
failure_condition: If the combined trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances.
